threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still refer to Clissold Park as a 'hidden gem' instead of just the meeting point for every kid's birthday party.
Last night on the 333, I saw a guy in a full tuxedo arguing with a mannequin he named 'Veronica'. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy repairing his bike while a woman juggled oranges at the corner stand—only in Neukölln do you get that level of multitasking.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can catch a drag queen performing in a laundromat here. Only in Neukölln will you find the mundane turned fabulous.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by their ability to navigate Muni's unpredictable schedule without a second thought—like it's part of their DNA.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the 14 bus riders with an accordion yesterday. The Muni vibe is something else—only in the Mission can you get a free concert while waiting!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that locals don’t queue for xurros at the popular spots—it's all about the hidden gems in the side streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still trying to order a 'café con leche' without stumbling over pronunciation, looking at the menu like it’s a crossword puzzle.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'River Lea' instead of 'Lea' like the locals do. That and you’re not layering yet for the unpredictable weather!
Saw a guy on the 133 last night singing opera. It was oddly beautiful, but everyone pretended not to notice. Only in Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I found a café with a cat lounging on the bar counter, and no one batted an eye. That’s when I knew I truly belonged.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how in Neukölln, old pubs turn into art galleries overnight? One day it's a beer hall, next it's an exhibition space. Just like that.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they can name the taco truck in front of the 24th St. BART and have a favorite salsa from there.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni driver singing along to the radio yesterday. Didn't miss a beat while navigating a packed 14-Mission. Classic SF vibe.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think all the plaça are for lounging. But on weekends, locals sweep in for a quick café, then rush off to the next barra.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still staring at the metro map like it’s a treasure map, while locals zip past them, knowing the shortcuts.
